satire_attempt: Internal Architecture Spreadsheet Now 'Older Than Any API Style Guide.' Sources confirm the spreadsheet, which scores contracts against nine Krishna-derived properties, was discovered to also be 'sarva-gata' (present in every Slack channel) and 'sthāṇu' (untouched since Q2). Engineering managers report the spreadsheet's 'sanātana' property (primordial) is misleading because it was actually generated by an intern in November. The spreadsheet could not be reached for comment.
verdict_reason: The satire only lands if you ignore the falsifiability field, which preempts the 'spreadsheet-as-virtue' read: 'A contract is not eternal because it carries a version number; it is eternal if it actually survives the next migration. It is not all-pervading because it is policy that everybody honour it; it is all-pervading if every service in fact honours it under audit.' The verse's nine properties are reality-tests, not labels. Maya's closing line in the scenario — 'Run any candidate against it; the ones that pass are the ones the company can actually build on' — operationalises this. The nine-property checklist is a falsifier-driven discipline, not an honour roll. To make the satire land, you would have to disagree with the meta-claim that some contracts genuinely meet the nine properties under audit, which is a real engineering assertion you can argue with rather than mock. PASS.
